United Kingdom.qualification_recognition:
QUALIFICATION RECOGNITION — UNITED KINGDOM

The UK does not use a centralised equivalency database. Each university lists requirements for international qualifications on their own website. The following are universally recognised:

CAMBRIDGE A-LEVELS (3 subjects): Primary UK entry qualification. Accepted by every university. Grades range from AAB (mid-tier) to A*A*A (Oxbridge). Subject choice matters — Engineering requires Maths and Physics, Medicine requires Chemistry and Biology.

IB DIPLOMA: Universally accepted including Oxbridge. Typically 32-38 points overall. Oxford/Cambridge require 38-40 points with 7,7,6 or 7,7,7 at HL.

INDIAN CBSE / ISC CLASS 12 (5 subjects): Accepted at most UK universities. Typically 85-95% overall for competitive universities. Subject-specific requirements apply — PCM for Engineering. Some highly competitive programmes may require additional evidence.

AP EXAMS + US HIGH SCHOOL DIPLOMA: Accepted at most universities including Russell Group. 3-5 AP exams at grade 4 or 5 typically required. US Diploma must be from accredited school.

UAE / GULF INTERNATIONAL SCHOOL A-LEVELS OR IB: Fully accepted. Same grade requirements as standard A-Levels or IB.

UAE TAWJIHI: Accepted at some universities — check individually. May require Foundation Year. Many competitive Russell Group programmes prefer A-Levels or IB alongside Tawjihi.

PAKISTANI A-LEVELS (CAMBRIDGE): Directly accepted at all UK universities. Same as UK A-Levels.

PAKISTANI FSc / HSSC: Accepted at many UK universities. Russell Group may prefer A-Levels or IB. Foundation Year available.

BANGLADESHI HSC: Accepted at many UK universities. Foundation Year available if grades below threshold. Cambridge A-Levels from Bangladesh fully accepted.

SRI LANKA A/L (3 subjects): Well-recognised across UK universities. ABB or above needed for Russell Group.

CAMBRIDGE IGCSE: NOT a university entry qualification. IGCSE is Grade 10 equivalent. Must complete A-Levels or IB after IGCSE before applying to UK universities.

FOUNDATION YEAR: A preparatory Year 0 offered by most UK universities for international students whose qualifications do not meet direct entry requirements. Completing with required grade guarantees or strongly supports entry to the linked degree. Duration 1 academic year. Cost typically £14,000-£20,000. Available at almost all UK universities including Manchester, UCL, Leeds, and through INTO, Kaplan, Navitas partnerships.

TWO-GATE SYSTEM:
Gate 1 — Eligibility to apply: Your school leaving certificate meets the minimum entry threshold. Checked by UCAS qualification equivalency and individual university requirements page.
Gate 2 — Competitive for an offer: Your specific grades, personal statement, admissions test scores, interview performance, and predicted grades. Checked independently by each university.

ENGLISH LANGUAGE REQUIREMENTS:
All UK degrees taught in English. Non-native English speakers must prove proficiency.
IELTS Academic: 6.0-6.5 overall (no band below 5.5) for standard programmes. 7.0-7.5 overall for Medicine, Law, and competitive programmes.
TOEFL iBT: 80-90 overall standard. 100-110 for Medicine/Law.
PTE Academic: 58-65 overall standard. 69-76 for Medicine/Law.
Exemptions: Nationals of majority English-speaking countries. Students who completed previous qualification entirely in English — but even from English-medium schools, most UK universities still require formal IELTS/TOEFL. Confirm with each university.

INDIAN STUDENT SPECIFICS:
No APS certificate required for UK — this is Germany-specific only.
No separate document verification system.
Even from English-medium international school, most UK universities require formal IELTS (6.5+ standard; 7.0+ for Medicine/Law).
For Medicine: Must sit UCAT by September before 15 October UCAS deadline.
For Law at Oxford/LSE/UCL/KCL/Durham/Bristol/Nottingham/Glasgow/SOAS: Must sit LNAT.
TB test required before visa application for students from India, Pakistan, Bangladesh.

FINANCIAL PROOF FOR STUDENT VISA:
London universities: £1,529 per month x 9 months = £13,761 to show.
Outside London: £1,171 per month x 9 months = £10,539 to show.
Must have held this amount for 28 consecutive days before visa application.
No blocked account required — regular bank account statement is sufficient.
Visa fee: £524. Immigration Health Surcharge: £776 per year paid upfront.
TB test required for India, Pakistan, Bangladesh passport holders.
Graduate Route Visa after graduation: 2 years for Bachelor's/Master's, 3 years for PhD.
United Kingdom.admission_checklist:
APPLICATION PORTAL AND PROCESS
UCAS (ucas.com) is the ONLY portal for all UK undergraduate applications. Application fee: £22.50 (one choice) or £27.50 (two or more choices). Maximum 5 university choices (4 for Medicine, Dentistry, Veterinary Science). Cannot apply to both Oxford AND Cambridge in the same year. All 5 choices submitted simultaneously — universities do not see where else you applied. UCAS opens September each year for following September entry.

CRITICAL DEADLINES
15 October: MANDATORY for Oxford, Cambridge, and ALL Medicine/Dentistry/Veterinary Science at any UK university. Missing this means waiting a full year for these courses. 29 January: Equal consideration deadline for all other courses. 30 June: Final international deadline. After this, only Clearing available. Clearing opens August after A-Level results — unfilled places advertised, students who missed offers can apply.

OXFORD AND CAMBRIDGE — ADDITIONAL REQUIREMENTS
Both require admissions tests AND interviews. Interviews held in December (Oxbridge). Approximately 40-50% of applicants are invited to interview. Interview performance is heavily weighted. Oxford: most courses require written admissions test in October. Cambridge: College Admissions Assessment at interview stage for most subjects. Foundation Year: NOT available at Oxford or Cambridge — direct entry only.

FOUNDATION YEAR PATHWAY
Available at most UK universities for students whose qualifications do not meet direct entry requirements. Completing with required grade guarantees or strongly supports entry to the linked degree programme. Duration: 1 academic year. Cost: £14,000–20,000 for the foundation year. Scotland universities offer 4-year degrees (broader first year) vs 3 years in England, Wales, and Northern Ireland.

ADMISSIONS TESTS — SUBJECT AND UNIVERSITY SPECIFIC
UCAT: Mandatory for Medicine and Dentistry at almost all UK medical schools. Sit July–September BEFORE 15 October UCAS deadline. Register at ucat.ac.uk. Fee: £70 (EU/UK) or £115 (international). LNAT: Required for Law at Oxford, Cambridge, UCL, LSE, KCL, Durham, Bristol, Nottingham, Glasgow, SOAS only. Fee: £75 (UK/EU) or £120 (international). ESAT: Cambridge Engineering and Natural Sciences, Imperial Engineering. October. Register at admissionstesting.org. Fee £75. MAT: Oxford Mathematics and Computer Science. Also Imperial and Warwick. October. Free. TMUA: Cambridge Economics and Computer Science. Also LSE, Warwick, Durham. October. Fee £75. STEP: Cambridge Mathematics — required as condition of offer. June. Free.

ENGLISH LANGUAGE REQUIREMENTS
IELTS Academic: 6.0–6.5 overall standard; 7.0–7.5 for Medicine/Law/Oxford/Cambridge. TOEFL iBT: 80–90 standard; 100–110 competitive programmes. Even English-medium school students usually require formal IELTS — confirm with each university.

PERSONAL STATEMENT
4,000 characters (transitioning to structured questions from 2026 entry). Must be ACADEMICALLY focused — explain why you love the subject. Do NOT write a US-style personal statement about personal qualities. One personal statement sent to all 5 universities — cannot customise per university.

FINANCIAL PROOF FOR STUDENT VISA
Proof of funds required: £1,529/month for London (9 months = £13,761). £1,171/month outside London (9 months = £10,539). Must hold funds for 28 consecutive days before applying. PLUS full first year tuition fee if not already paid. IHS (Immigration Health Surcharge): £776 per year paid upfront for full visa duration. For 3-year degree: approximately £2,328 total.

STUDENT VISA PROCEDURE
Apply for UK Student Visa AFTER receiving unconditional offer or confirmed place. Requires CAS (Confirmation of Acceptance for Studies) number from university. Apply online at gov.uk/student-visa. Processing time: approximately 3 weeks. TB test required for applicants from India, Pakistan, Bangladesh, Nigeria, Ghana, Philippines — must be done at approved clinic before visa application. Graduate Route Visa: 2 years post-study work (Bachelor's/Master's), 3 years (PhD). Available at all Licensed Sponsor universities.

OFFICIAL SOURCES FOR VERIFICATION
ucas.com — application portal and deadlines
ucat.ac.uk — UCAT registration
lnat.ac.uk — LNAT registration
admissionstesting.org — ESAT, MAT, TMUA registration
gov.uk/student-visa — UK Student Visa guidance
ukcisa.org.uk — UK Council for International Student Affairs
gov.uk/healthcare-immigration-application — IHS surcharge

Germany.qualification_recognition:
QUALIFICATION RECOGNITION — GERMANY

The single most important question Germany asks: Is your school leaving certificate equivalent to the German Abitur (HZB)? Everything else flows from this answer. Check your specific certificate at DAAD database (daad.de) or anabin (anabin.kmk.org).

FOUR ADMISSION CATEGORIES:

CATEGORY 1 — DIRECT GENERAL ADMISSION: Certificate fully equivalent to German Abitur. Apply directly to any programme.
Who qualifies: IB Diploma, Cambridge A-Levels (3 subjects), most EU school certificates, strong Indian CBSE Class 12 (5 subjects), American High School Diploma + SAT/ACT, South African NSC with University Endorsement.
Still required: Language certificate (TestDaF or IELTS), APS certificate if Indian/Chinese/Vietnamese nationality, TestAS if required by target university.

CATEGORY 2 — DIRECT SUBJECT-RESTRICTED ADMISSION: Certificate recognised but only for a specific subject area matching school specialisation. Less common for international students from India, UAE, Pakistan.

CATEGORY 3 — STUDIENKOLLEG ROUTE: Certificate not fully equivalent to Abitur. Must complete 1-year preparatory course in Germany first.
Who falls here: Some Indian Class 12 certificates with only 4 subjects, Pakistani HSSC in some cases, Bangladeshi HSC, many Middle Eastern national certificates.
Process: Apply to Studienkolleg, complete 1-year programme (T-track Engineering, M-track Medicine, W-track Business, G-track Humanities), sit and pass Feststellungsprüfung (FSP), then apply to degree programme normally.
Studienkolleg is a legitimate recognised government-funded pathway — not a failure state. Adds approximately 1 year to timeline.

CATEGORY 4 — NOT YET ELIGIBLE: Students with only 10 years of school (O-Levels/IGCSE/Grade 10 only without A-Levels or equivalent). Options: Complete A-Levels or IB then reapply, or complete first year of a recognised bachelor's degree elsewhere then apply as transfer.

GERMAN NATIONALS STUDYING ABROAD — DIFFERENT RULES:
German passport holders are treated as domestic students.
No APS certificate — APS is only for Indian, Chinese, Vietnamese nationals.
No blocked account — German citizens are exempt.
No visa required — free movement as EU citizen.
No uni-assist required — apply directly through university portal or hochschulkompass.de.
For Medicine: compete in full domestic pool, not the limited international quota.
BUT: Still need HZB equivalency check for foreign certificate (A-Levels, IB, US Diploma). A-Levels and IB are recognised. UAE Tawjihi may still require Studienkolleg even for German nationals.
Language: If schooled entirely in English abroad, TestDaF Level 4 or DSH-2 required even for German nationals. DSD II certificate from approved school satisfies this completely.
BAföG financial aid may be available — check bafoeg.bmbf.de.

NC (NUMERUS CLAUSUS) GRADE SYSTEM:
In Germany 1.0 is the BEST grade, 4.0 is the worst.
NC 1.0-1.2 (top 3%): Medicine, Dentistry, Veterinary Medicine.
NC 1.3-1.8 (top 10-15%): Psychology, Pharmacy, Law at top universities.
NC 1.9-2.5 (top 25-30%): Engineering, Computer Science, Architecture, most Business.
NC 2.6-3.0: Many Humanities, Languages, Social Sciences at regional universities.
No NC (Zulassungsfrei): Mathematics, Physics, many regional STEM and Humanities — anyone meeting HZB minimum can enrol.
Important: NC published online is the grade of the LAST person admitted the previous semester — not a fixed minimum. It changes every year. Always apply even if grade is slightly above the published NC.

APS CERTIFICATE — MANDATORY FOR INDIAN NATIONALS:
If student holds Indian passport — APS certificate is non-negotiable regardless of where they studied. Even if studied entire life in Oman, UAE, or anywhere outside India.
What APS does: German Embassy in New Delhi verifies academic certificates are genuine.
Where to apply: German Embassy New Delhi APS India office — by post or in person.
Timeline: 4-8 weeks. Apply well before university application deadline.
Fee: approximately INR 15,000-20,000.
Also required for: Chinese nationals and Vietnamese nationals.
Not required for: all other nationalities including Pakistani, Bangladeshi, UAE national.

LANGUAGE REQUIREMENTS:
German-taught programmes (approximately 90% of all undergraduate courses): Require C1 German — TestDaF Level 4 in all 4 skills, or DSH-2, or Goethe-Zertifikat C2, or telc Deutsch C1 Hochschule, or DSD II from approved school. Realistically 18-24 months of serious study from zero.

English-taught programmes (growing — Engineering, Business, CS): IELTS 6.5 overall or TOEFL 80+. Students from English-medium international schools usually already meet this.

Private universities (IU, CBS, SRH): English only for most programmes. More flexible admission criteria. Lower barrier to entry.

BLOCKED ACCOUNT AND VISA:
All international students (non-German, non-EU) require blocked account (Sperrkonto) showing €11,904 before visa. Can only withdraw €992 per month once in Germany. Providers: Fintiba, Expatrio, Deutsche Bank.
Semester fee/contribution: €250-400 every 6 months — administrative fee, not tuition.
Most public universities have zero tuition fees for all students.
Germany.admission_checklist:
APPLICATION PORTAL AND PROCESS
Primary portal: uni-assist e.V. (uni-assist.de). Application fee €75 per university. Most international students apply here. Deadlines: 15 July for winter semester (October start), 15 January for summer semester (April start). Some universities accept direct applications via their own portal — always check individual university website. Medicine, Dentistry, Pharmacy, Veterinary Medicine: apply via hochschulstart.de (separate national portal, up to 6 universities at once). Verify on official page whether the target university uses uni-assist or direct application.

TWO-GATE SYSTEM — CRITICAL CONCEPT
Gate 1: Can you study in Germany? Answered by HZB/Abitur equivalency (admission category 1-4). Gate 2: Can you get into THIS specific programme? Answered by NC cutoff, TestAS, language test, TMS score. Passing Gate 1 does NOT guarantee passing Gate 2. Both gates must be passed before enrolment. Verify both separately for each target university and programme.

APS CERTIFICATE — PROCEDURE
Mandatory for Indian, Chinese, and Vietnamese passport holders only. Applies regardless of which country the student studied in — an Indian passport holder who studied in UAE still needs APS. Apply at German Embassy Academic Department in home country. India: German Embassy New Delhi or consulates in Mumbai, Chennai, Kolkata. Official link: india.diplo.de. Processing time: 4-8 weeks. Fee: approximately INR 15,000-20,000 (verify current fee at india.diplo.de). Must be obtained BEFORE submitting university application. Without APS, application is rejected regardless of grades or admission category. Not required for Pakistani, Bangladeshi, UAE, or other nationalities.

TESTAS — UNIVERSITY-SPECIFIC, NOT NATIONALLY MANDATORY
TestAS is NOT required by a central national authority. Each university decides independently. Three possible uses: (1) MANDATORY — application rejected without it; (2) OPTIONAL — improves ranking if submitted; (3) NOT USED — irrelevant for this university. Always check the specific university's international admissions page. Three modules: Engineering/Natural Sciences, Economics/Social Sciences, Humanities. Fee approximately €65. Register at testas.de. Results valid 5 years. Does not apply to EU/EEA students. Does not apply to most private universities. Particularly important for Studienkolleg graduates applying to competitive programmes.

TMS — MEDICAL APTITUDE TEST (MEDICINE STREAM ONLY)
Not legally mandatory but practically essential for Medicine, Dentistry, Pharmacy, and Veterinary Medicine at public universities. Most public medical faculties include TMS score in selection criteria. Conducted IN GERMAN ONLY — not available in English. Held once per year (typically May). Fee €75 in Germany, €100 abroad. Register via Heidelberg University. Official link: tms-info.org. A strong TMS score can compensate for a slightly weaker school grade. Non-EU students may be asked for TestAS instead of TMS at some faculties — verify with target university.

BLOCKED ACCOUNT PROCEDURE
Required before student visa application. Amount: €11,904 for 12 months (€992/month withdrawal limit) — as of 2025. Approved providers: Fintiba (fintiba.com), Expatrio (expatrio.com), Deutsche Bank. Process: apply online after receiving university admission letter (Zulassungsbescheid), transfer full amount, receive confirmation letter. Timing: open ONLY after receiving admission letter, BEFORE applying for visa. Confirmation letter is a mandatory visa document. German nationals and EU citizens are exempt — this requirement applies to international students only. Cannot withdraw full amount at once — monthly limit enforced.

HEALTH INSURANCE
Mandatory for university enrolment. Must be German statutory health insurance (gesetzliche Krankenversicherung). Major providers: TK (Techniker Krankenkasse), AOK, Barmer, DAK. Student tariff: approximately €110-130 per month (2025). Private international health insurance not accepted for enrolment purposes. Obtain before registering at university. Provider issues enrolment confirmation letter which is required for matriculation.

STUDENT VISA PROCEDURE
Visa type: National Visa Type D for enrolled students. Apply at German Embassy or Consulate in country of RESIDENCE (not necessarily country of citizenship). Processing time: 8-12 weeks. Book visa appointment early — slots fill quickly especially at German Embassy Muscat and German Embassy New Delhi. Required documents at visa appointment: university admission letter (Zulassungsbescheid), blocked account confirmation letter, APS certificate (India/China/Vietnam only), language certificate (TestDaF or IELTS), health insurance confirmation, financial proof, valid passport, biometric photos, completed visa application form.

NC SYSTEM AND PROGRAMME COMPETITIVENESS
NC = grade of last admitted student in previous semester. Scale: 1.0 (best) to 4.0 (worst). Published NC is NOT a hard cutoff — it changes every year. Always apply even if grade is slightly above (worse than) the published NC. Typical ranges: Medicine/Dentistry 1.0-1.2; Psychology/Pharmacy/Law at top universities 1.3-1.8; Engineering/CS at good universities 1.9-2.5; Humanities/regional universities 2.6-3.0. Zulassungsfrei (no NC): some programmes have free admission. Alternative to NC: Wartesemester (waiting semesters) — each year waiting after school adds priority points.

TUITION AND FEES
Public universities: zero tuition for most programmes. Exception: Baden-Württemberg state charges non-EU international students approximately €3,000 per semester — this is a significant exception affecting universities including Heidelberg, Stuttgart, KIT. Semester fee: €250-400 every 6 months at all public universities — mandatory, covers administration, often includes public transport pass. Private universities: €5,000-20,000 per year. Always verify current fee status directly with target university.

OFFICIAL SOURCES FOR VERIFICATION
uni-assist.de — application portal, deadlines, document requirements
daad.de/en — authoritative for all programme and admission information
hochschulstart.de — Medicine, Dentistry, Pharmacy, Veterinary Medicine applications
testas.de — TestAS registration and test centres
tms-info.org — TMS medical aptitude test
india.diplo.de — APS certificate for Indian nationals
aps.org.cn — APS certificate for Chinese nationals
anabin.kmk.org — qualification recognition database
hochschulkompass.de — university and programme search
fintiba.com and expatrio.com — blocked account providers
study-in-germany.de — official German government student portal

India.qualification_recognition:
QUALIFICATION RECOGNITION — INDIA

FOREIGN NATIONAL ROUTING NOTE: If the student holds no Indian passport and no OCI card, admission routes are completely different — ICCR scholarship, Study in India portal, or direct university application. JEE/NEET rules differ for foreign nationals. Do not apply NRI or resident Indian rules to foreign nationals.

STUDENT STATUS CLASSIFICATION — MOST IMPORTANT FIRST STEP:
NRI (Non-Resident Indian): Indian citizen (Indian passport) residing outside India. Eligible for JEE/NEET/CLAT as Indian student. May be directed to NRI quota seats. Eligible for DASA if studied Class 11 and 12 outside India for past 2 years.
OCI (Overseas Citizen of India): Foreign national of Indian origin holding OCI card — NOT Indian citizenship. For JEE Advanced: treated as foreign national, can appear directly without JEE Main, eligible for supernumerary seats. For NEET and CLAT: eligible. For DASA: eligible.
PIO (Person of Indian Origin): Functionally same as OCI for admission purposes.
NRI-Sponsored: Indian student in India whose studies are sponsored by an NRI blood relative. Eligible for NRI quota at CLAT NLUs and private medical colleges.
Gulf-specific: Indian passport holder in Oman/UAE/Saudi/Kuwait/Bahrain/Qatar is NRI. Child studying at international school in Muscat/Dubai with Indian passport is NRI. Child born abroad with UAE/Omani passport with Indian-origin parents is OCI.

WHICH QUALIFICATIONS ARE ACCEPTED:
CBSE Class 12: Fully recognised for all Indian entrance exams. JEE requires 75% aggregate in PCM. NEET requires 50% in PCB. CLAT requires 45% in Class 12. CBSE from overseas Indian schools (UAE, Oman, Kuwait) is fully recognised — treated identically to CBSE India.
ISC (Class 12): Fully recognised for JEE, NEET, CLAT, CUET. ISC percentage generally slightly lower than CBSE due to stricter marking.
IB DIPLOMA: Recognised by AIU. Eligible for JEE with Physics Chemistry Maths in diploma. Eligible for NEET with Biology Chemistry Physics. Eligible for CLAT. Standard Level and Higher Level both count.
CAMBRIDGE A-LEVELS: Recognised by AIU. Eligible for JEE with Physics Chemistry Maths at A-Level. Eligible for NEET with Biology Chemistry Physics. Eligible for CLAT.
CAMBRIDGE IGCSE: NOT sufficient alone. IGCSE is Class 10 equivalent. Must complete Class 12 equivalent (A-Level, IB, CBSE, ISC, State Board) before applying to any Indian university.
STATE BOARDS: Recognised for JEE, NEET, CLAT. State quota seats may prefer state board students.
US HIGH SCHOOL DIPLOMA: Recognised through AIU equivalence. AP courses in relevant subjects demonstrate subject eligibility.

THE 75% RULE FOR JEE — TWO SEPARATE GATES:
Gate 1 — Appearing for JEE Main: No minimum percentage required to sit the exam.
Gate 2 — NIT/IIIT/CFTI admission: Must have 75% aggregate in Class 12 equivalent (65% for SC/ST). Without 75% can still use JEE rank for IITs via JEE Advanced and private engineering colleges.
For IB Diploma: 75% equivalent is approximately 34 out of 45 points. Top-20 percentile of IB cohort globally is an alternative route.
For A-Level: Only 3-4 subjects normally studied. Need 5-subject aggregate — typically 3 A-Levels plus 2 AS-Level or IGCSE subjects. Grades: A*=90-95%, A=80-85%, B=70-75%, C=60-65% approximate JoSAA conversion. Aim for ABB minimum, AAA for safety.
State Code of Eligibility: NRI students who completed Class 12 outside India must select state code based on permanent Indian address in passport — NOT current country of residence.

IB 3-SCIENCE PROBLEM FOR NEET:
NEET requires Physics, Chemistry, AND Biology all present in Class 12. IB Group 4 typically allows only one science subject — many IB students have Biology and Chemistry but not Physics, or Physics and Chemistry but not Biology.
Three solutions:
1. Take missing science as IB certificate (non-diploma) subject. Official IB record. Accepted by most Indian medical colleges. Discuss with IB Coordinator at least 1-2 years before exam.
2. Apply to IB Cardiff for non-standard diploma arrangement allowing 3 Group 4 sciences — very limited, must apply 18 months before exam.
3. Switch to CBSE with PCB subjects — most reliable if MBBS in India is the confirmed goal.
A-Level English dropout problem: NEET requires English as one of Class 12 subjects. Students who chose only science A-Levels and dropped English after IGCSE may not meet NEET eligibility. Keep at least AS-Level English. IGCSE English Grade C or above sometimes accepted — check NTA guidelines in year of application.

AIU EQUIVALENCE CERTIFICATE:
Required for: NEET admission at private/deemed medical colleges, some state university engineering counselling, some JoSAA NIT counsellors for percentage verification.
NOT required for: JEE Main registration, CLAT registration, Ashoka/FLAME/OP Jindal direct applications, BITS Pilani ISA process.
How to get: Apply at evaluation.aiu.ac.in. Documents: original mark sheet, passing certificate, school leaving certificate, English translation if needed. Cost: INR 3,000-5,000. Processing: 2-4 weeks. Apply at least 6 weeks before any deadline.

RESERVATION SYSTEM IMPACT:
India has constitutionally mandated reservations: SC 15%, ST 7.5%, OBC 27%, EWS 10%. Total reserved 59.5%. General/Open category 40.5%.
NRI/OCI/PIO students are NOT eligible for any reservation category. They compete in general/open category or under NRI-specific quotas.

FOUNDATION AND BRIDGE ROUTES:
No Studienkolleg equivalent in India. However:
Private universities (Ashoka, FLAME, OP Jindal, Krea) accept IB/A-Level directly — no NCERT entrance exam needed. Best fit for NRI students on international curricula.
BITS Pilani ISA (International Students Admission): SAT accepted for international students instead of BITSAT. No JEE required for this pathway.
For JEE/NEET aspirants on IB/A-Level: supplementary NCERT-gap coaching is mandatory. JEE syllabus is almost entirely NCERT-based. IB/A-Level provides strong conceptual foundation but does not cover all NCERT topics. Inorganic Chemistry is the biggest gap. Start supplementary coaching in Class 11.
India.admission_checklist:
ENTRANCE EXAM SYSTEM — NO SINGLE PORTAL
India has no central undergraduate application portal. Admissions governed by entrance exams. Different exams for different streams: JEE for Engineering at IITs/NITs, NEET for Medicine/Dentistry, CLAT for Law (NLUs), CUET for Central Universities, CAT for IIM Management, BITSAT for BITS Pilani. State universities have additional state-level exams.

KEY ENTRANCE EXAMS
JEE Main: National engineering entrance conducted twice/year (January and April) by NTA. Required for NITs, IIITs, GFTIs. Also qualifying exam for JEE Advanced. Register at jeemain.nta.nic.in. JEE Advanced: Exclusively for 23 IITs. Only top 250,000 JEE Main scorers eligible. Once per year (May/June). Register at jeeadv.ac.in. NEET-UG: Single national exam for all MBBS, BDS, and allied health. Once per year (May) by NTA. Over 2 million candidates. Register at neet.nta.nic.in. CLAT: Law entrance for 26 National Law Universities. December. NLU Delhi uses AILET separately. Register at consortiumofnlus.ac.in. CUET-UG: Required since 2022 for Central Universities including Delhi University, JNU, BHU. Register at cuet.samarth.ac.in. BITSAT: BITS Pilani own entrance exam for engineering — separate from JEE. Register at bitsadmission.com.

PRIVATE UNIVERSITIES — DIRECT APPLICATION
VIT Vellore, Manipal, SRM, Amity, OP Jindal, Ashoka do NOT require JEE — apply directly through their own portals. Fees in USD for international students: USD 2,500–8,000/year at government universities (NITs); USD 4,000–15,000/year at top private universities; MBBS at private colleges USD 10,000–25,000/year. Still significantly more affordable than UK, USA, or Australia.

DASA/CIWG FOR NRI AND OCI STUDENTS
DASA: For NRI/OCI students who studied Classes 11 and 12 OUTSIDE India. Admission to NITs, IIITs based on JEE Main score. Register at dasanit.org after JEE Main results (June–July). CIWG Gulf Quota: Reserved within DASA for children of Indian workers in UAE, Saudi, Qatar, Oman, Kuwait, Bahrain, Iraq, Iran. Parent must have valid employment visa in Gulf country. Significantly less competition than general JEE counselling.

AIU EQUIVALENCE CERTIFICATE
Required for foreign school certificates (A-Levels, IB, CBSE from abroad) at many Indian institutions. Apply at evaluation.aiu.ac.in. Documents: original mark sheet, passing certificate, school leaving certificate, translated documents if not in English, passport copy. Cost: approximately INR 3,000–5,000. Processing: 2–4 weeks. Apply at least 6 weeks before any deadline.

STUDENT VISA (FOREIGN NATIONALS ONLY)
Indian passport holders (NRI): no visa required. OCI/PIO with foreign passports and foreign nationals: need Student Visa. SII Student ID MANDATORY from 2025-26: Register at studyinindia.gov.in BEFORE applying for visa — SII ID must be quoted on visa application form. Apply for Student Visa at indianvisaonline.gov.in. Documents: SII Student ID, admission letter, tuition fee payment proof, bank statement, passport photos. FRRO registration mandatory within 14 days of arrival at indianfrro.gov.in.

ICCR SCHOLARSHIP (FOREIGN NATIONALS)
Fully funded Government of India scholarship for nationals of 160+ countries. Apply through A2A portal at a2ascholarships.iccr.gov.in via Indian Mission in your country. Portal opens February each year, deadline approximately 30 April.

OFFICIAL SOURCES FOR VERIFICATION
jeemain.nta.nic.in — JEE Main
jeeadv.ac.in — JEE Advanced
neet.nta.nic.in — NEET-UG
dasanit.org — DASA/CIWG admission
evaluation.aiu.ac.in — AIU equivalence certificate
studyinindia.gov.in — SII portal (mandatory for foreign students)
indianvisaonline.gov.in — Student Visa application
indianfrro.gov.in — FRRO registration
bitsadmission.com — BITSAT for BITS Pilani

USA.qualification_recognition:
QUALIFICATION RECOGNITION — UNITED STATES

The USA does not have a single national certificate equivalency database. Each university independently assesses international qualifications. Admission is HOLISTIC — grades matter but so do essays, extracurriculars, recommendations, and character. This is the single most important distinction from all European systems.

HOLISTIC ADMISSIONS — THE CORE DIFFERENCE:
US selective universities do NOT admit purely on grades. They assess: academic transcript and course rigor, standardised tests (SAT/ACT where required), personal statement/essay, supplemental essays, extracurricular activities, letters of recommendation, demonstrated interest, and character/fit. A student with 98% grades can be rejected from Harvard while a student with 88% grades who started a non-profit is admitted. Context, story, and authenticity matter.

WHICH QUALIFICATIONS ARE ACCEPTED:
IB DIPLOMA: Universally recognised and highly regarded. IB HL grades of 5-7 may earn college credit. Strong IB (35-45 points with high HL grades) is competitive for top universities.
CAMBRIDGE A-LEVELS (2-3 subjects): Widely accepted. High marks (A*, A, B) demonstrate college-level work. 3 A-Levels with strong grades are very competitive. Include IGCSE/GCSE grades alongside A-Levels.
AP EXAMS (5 or more): Most competitive option for international students. Shows familiarity with US curriculum. AP scores 4-5 in 5+ subjects is excellent. May earn college credit.
INDIAN CBSE / ISC CLASS 12: Accepted at all US universities. 90%+ overall generally competitive. 95%+ in STEM for competitive STEM programmes. Top CBSE students are regularly admitted to Ivy League.
PAKISTANI FSc / CAMBRIDGE A-LEVELS: Accepted. Cambridge A-Levels from Pakistan treated same as UK A-Levels. High FSc scores (80%+) or strong A-Level grades (ABB or better) required.
SRI LANKA A/L: Accepted at most US universities. Strong performance in 3 subjects.
UAE / GULF INTERNATIONAL SCHOOL A-LEVELS OR IB: Fully accepted — same as standard qualifications.
CAMBRIDGE IGCSE: Not a university entry qualification alone. Include IGCSE grades as part of your full academic history but must complete A-Levels or IB to qualify.
EGYPTIAN THANAWEYA AMMA: Accepted at many universities — 90%+ typically required. Check individually.
US HIGH SCHOOL DIPLOMA: Most straightforward — same as domestic applicants.

GRADE CONVERSION:
US universities do NOT use a standardised global conversion table. Each admissions office makes its own assessment. Approximate: 90%+ ≈ 4.0 GPA, 80-90% ≈ 3.3-3.9, A-Level A* ≈ 4.0, A ≈ 3.7-4.0, IB 40+ ≈ 4.0, 35-39 ≈ 3.7-3.9. Some universities use credential evaluation services (WES, ECE) for official GPA equivalent.

APPLICATION PLATFORMS:
Common Application: Used by 1,000+ universities. Most universities use this.
UC Application: University of California system only — separate from Common App.
MIT Application: Completely separate — MIT does not use Common App.
Coalition Application: Used by select universities as alternative to Common App.
Apply to the right platform for each university — do not assume all use Common App.

STANDARDISED TESTS — SAT / ACT:
Test-optional at most schools now. When submitted: SAT 1450+ or ACT 32+ for competitive schools. MIT, Yale, Dartmouth reinstated requirements in 2024-2025 — check current policy per university. For test-optional schools, submit only if score is strong (SAT 1400+).

ENGLISH LANGUAGE REQUIREMENTS:
TOEFL iBT: 79-100 standard. 100-110+ for elite universities (Harvard min 100, MIT 90+).
IELTS Academic: 6.5-7.0 standard. 7.0-7.5+ for competitive universities.
Duolingo English Test: 105-120 standard. 120-135+ for competitive schools.
Exemptions: Native English speakers. English-medium schooling 4+ years (varies by school). A-Level English grade C+ or IB English A grade 4+. Even with exemptions, many top universities (Harvard, MIT, Princeton) prefer TOEFL/IELTS scores — a strong score reinforces the application.
For Indian passport holders studying abroad: TOEFL/IELTS generally required. Aim for TOEFL 100+ or IELTS 7.0+ for competitive schools.

FINANCIAL AID AND COST:
Most US universities offer very limited financial aid to international students. Exception: highly endowed elite private universities that meet 100% of demonstrated financial need for all admitted students including international — Harvard, Yale, Princeton, MIT, Dartmouth, Amherst, Williams, Pomona, Bowdoin, Middlebury. If financial aid is needed, focus applications on need-blind universities that meet 100% of need.
F-1 Student Visa: Required for all international students. Apply after receiving I-20 from university. Begin process immediately after acceptance — I-20 processing and visa appointment booking can take weeks.
OPT (Optional Practical Training): 12 months of work authorisation after graduation. Apply through university international office to USCIS — processing 3-5 months, apply early.
STEM OPT extension: 24 additional months for STEM degree holders — total 36 months post-graduation work rights.
USA.admission_checklist:
APPLICATION SYSTEMS
Common Application (commonapp.org): used by 1,000+ universities. One application, multiple schools — each may require supplemental essays. Coalition Application: approximately 150 selective colleges — some schools accept both. UC Application (universityofcalifornia.edu): ONLY for 10 University of California campuses — completely separate from Common App. Apply November 1–30. MIT Application: MIT has its own portal — does not use Common App.

CRITICAL DEADLINES
Early Decision (ED — binding commitment): November 1 or 15. If admitted, you must attend — cannot compare other financial aid offers. Higher admission rates at many schools. Only apply ED if this is your definitive first choice AND you can afford the aid package. Early Action (EA — non-binding): November 1 or 15. Restrictive EA at Harvard, Stanford, Princeton, Yale — cannot apply EA elsewhere. Regular Decision (RD): January 1 or 15. Notifications March–April. National Decision Day: May 1 — final commitment deadline. UC Application: November 1–30.

HOLISTIC ADMISSIONS — FUNDAMENTALLY DIFFERENT FROM EUROPE
Grades are important but NOT the only factor. Essays, extracurriculars, recommendations, demonstrated interest, and character all heavily weighted. Personal statement: Common App 7 prompts, choose 1, 650 words maximum. Must reflect personality and story — NOT academically focused like UK. Supplemental essays: most universities require 1–5 university-specific additional essays. Demonstrated interest: some universities track campus visits, emails, event attendance — signals genuine interest.

SAT AND ACT STATUS
Many universities currently test-optional — verify each university's policy as it changes frequently. When required: SAT competitive score 1300+ (public); 1450–1550+ (Ivy League). ACT: 28+ (public); 34–36 (Ivy League). Students can retake — schools typically take highest score.

FINANCIAL AID — CRITICAL DISTINCTIONS
CSS Profile: Required by approximately 400 selective private universities for financial aid consideration — separate from Common App. File CSS Profile (cssprofile.org) in addition to FAFSA (US citizens/PR only). Need-blind vs need-aware: Need-blind universities do NOT consider ability to pay when making admission decision — Harvard, MIT, Princeton, Yale, Dartmouth, Amherst meet 100% of demonstrated financial need for ALL students including international. Need-aware universities consider finances — international students at need-aware schools receive very limited aid. Most US financial aid (FAFSA, Pell Grants) is NOT available to international students — rely on university merit scholarships.

ENGLISH LANGUAGE REQUIREMENTS
TOEFL iBT: 80–100 depending on university (most competitive: 100+). IELTS Academic: 6.5–7.0. Duolingo English Test: accepted by growing number (115–120 competitive). Students from English-medium schools may be exempt — confirm with each university.

F-1 VISA PROCEDURE
After accepting offer by May 1: university issues I-20 form. Pay SEVIS fee: $350 (mandatory before visa application). Apply for F-1 visa at US Embassy/Consulate. Can apply up to 365 days before programme start. Required documents: I-20, DS-160 form, SEVIS fee receipt, financial evidence, acceptance letter, transcripts, English test scores, passport photos, visa interview. Processing time: 2–4 months — apply early. 2025-26 note: increased processing times and scrutiny — apply earlier than usual.

POST-STUDY WORK
OPT: 12 months work authorisation after graduation. Apply to USCIS through university — processing 3–5 months, apply early. STEM OPT Extension: additional 24 months (36 months total) for STEM degrees. Employer must be E-Verify registered. After OPT: H-1B work visa lottery required.

OFFICIAL SOURCES FOR VERIFICATION
commonapp.org — Common Application
universityofcalifornia.edu — UC Application
travel.state.gov — F-1 visa guidance
uscis.gov — SEVIS, OPT, STEM OPT
ice.gov/sevis — SEVIS fee payment
cssprofile.collegeboard.org — CSS Profile for financial aid

Australia.qualification_recognition:
QUALIFICATION RECOGNITION — AUSTRALIA

STUDENT STATUS — THE GOLDEN RULE:
Australia has ONE binary rule: Australian Citizen or Permanent Resident = Domestic student (subsidised fees, HECS-HELP eligible). New Zealand Citizen = Domestic student (Trans-Tasman agreement). Everyone else = International student with full fees.
OCI cards, NRI status, Indian passports, UAE residency, Gulf work visas — NONE of these create any special category or quota in Australian university admissions. If you do not hold Australian citizenship or PR, you are an international student. Full stop. There are no NRI quotas, no OCI pathways, no Gulf worker exceptions.
Australian citizen living abroad (expat, grew up abroad): still treated as domestic student — HECS-HELP eligible, domestic fees.

NO NATIONAL ENTRANCE EXAM:
Australian universities assess international students on their home country qualifications. There is NO separate national entrance exam. Admission is based entirely on your school-leaving results and English test scores.

WHICH QUALIFICATIONS ARE ACCEPTED:
CAMBRIDGE A-LEVELS (3 subjects): Direct entry Year 1. Minimum 3 A-Levels. B grades or above for Go8.
IB DIPLOMA (full, 6 subjects): Direct entry. 24-45 IB points accepted. 38+ for Go8. 42+ for Medicine/Law.
INDIAN CBSE CLASS 12 (5 subjects): Direct entry. 85%+ for Go8. 75%+ for ATN universities. Foundation Year option below 70%.
ISC CLASS 12: Direct entry. Treated similarly to CBSE. Well-recognised.
PAKISTANI FSc / INTERMEDIATE: Accepted with strong marks (80%+). Some universities require Foundation Year.
UAE / GULF TAWJIHI (12-year): Accepted. 75%+ for Go8. English medium schooling or IELTS required.
INDIAN STATE BOARD: Accepted. 85%+ for Go8. Some universities may request AIU equivalence statement.
CAMBRIDGE IGCSE ONLY (Grade 10): NOT sufficient. Must complete A-Levels, IB, or Australian Foundation Year before applying. IGCSE is Grade 10 equivalent — not a university entry qualification.
CBSE CLASS 10 / O-LEVEL ONLY: Foundation Year required. Cannot apply to bachelor's degree directly.
AUSTRALIAN FOUNDATION YEAR: Completes the gap. Usually 8-12 months. Guaranteed pathway to affiliated university.

GRADE REQUIREMENTS BY TIER:
Go8 general degrees: ABB to AAA A-Levels, 35-42+ IB, 85-90%+ CBSE.
Go8 Medicine/Law: AAA or A*AA + UCAT/LSAT, 42+ IB + UCAT, 90%+ CBSE + UCAT.
ATN Universities: BBC to ABB A-Levels, 28-34 IB, 75-85% CBSE.
Other Universities: CCC to BCC A-Levels, 24-28 IB, 65-75% CBSE.
Medicine requires UCAT ANZ in addition to academic results — no exceptions.

FOUNDATION YEAR:
For students whose qualifications do not meet direct entry thresholds. 8-12 months. Guarantees pathway to affiliated university. Foundation entry threshold: 60%+ Class 11 equivalent, 5 IGCSE B grades.

ENGLISH LANGUAGE REQUIREMENTS:
General bachelor's: IELTS 6.0 overall, no band below 5.5. PTE 50 overall.
Business/Commerce: IELTS 6.5, no band below 6.0. PTE 58.
Law LLB: IELTS 7.0, no band below 6.5. PTE 65.
Medicine MBBS: IELTS 7.0 ALL bands 7.0. PTE 65 — all skills strict.
Nursing/Education: IELTS 7.0-7.5, all bands 7.0.

STUDENT VISA AND FINANCIAL REQUIREMENTS:
Subclass 500 Student Visa: Required for all international students studying full-time.
Financial evidence required by Department of Home Affairs: AUD 24,505 per year for student living costs. Plus full first-year tuition as per CoE. Plus AUD 8,574 per year for accompanying spouse. Plus AUD 3,670 per year per child.
Evidence accepted: Bank statements 3-6 months, fixed deposits, education loan letter, scholarship letter, parent payslips. Gulf AED/OMR/SAR bank statements accepted — convert to AUD equivalent.
OshC (Overseas Student Health Cover): Mandatory health insurance for all student visa holders. Must purchase before applying for visa. Providers: Medibank, Bupa, CBHS, AHM, NIB, Allianz.
Subclass 485 Post-Study Work Visa: 2-4+ years open work rights after graduation. Longer for regional campuses. No employer sponsorship needed. Critical for career and PR pathway.
Genuine Student (GS) Statement: Personal essay explaining study intentions, career plans, and ties to home country. Required for student visa application.
Australia.admission_checklist:
APPLICATION PROCESS — DIRECT TO UNIVERSITY
Australia has NO central application portal for international students. Apply directly to each university's international admissions office online. Application fee: AUD 50–100 per university (non-refundable). Each university has its own application portal. CRITICAL: Always verify the university is CRICOS registered before applying — check cricos.teqsa.gov.au. Only CRICOS-registered universities can legally enrol international students on student visas. Go8 (Group of Eight) universities are Australia's most research-intensive: University of Melbourne, ANU, University of Sydney, UNSW, Monash, UQ, UWA, Adelaide.

ADMISSIONS TESTS
UCAT ANZ: Mandatory for Medicine and Dentistry at Australian medical schools. Sit between July–September. Register at ucat.ac.nz. Same test as UK UCAT — shared platform.

ENGLISH LANGUAGE REQUIREMENTS
IELTS Academic: 6.0–7.0 depending on programme (6.5 standard; 7.0 for Medicine/Law/Teaching). PTE Academic: 50–65 depending on programme. TOEFL iBT: 60–94 depending on university. Valid 2 years. Send official score reports directly to universities.

OFFER AND ENROLMENT
Universities issue Conditional or Unconditional Offer. After accepting offer, pay tuition deposit (AUD 1,500–5,000). University issues CoE (Confirmation of Enrolment) — required before visa application. Do not apply for visa without CoE.

OSHC — MANDATORY HEALTH INSURANCE
Overseas Student Health Cover (OShC) is mandatory for ALL student visa holders. Must be purchased BEFORE applying for visa. Covers entire visa period. Approved providers: Medibank, Bupa, CBHS, AHM, NIB, Allianz. Approximate cost: AUD 500–700 per year. Policy must start before or on visa grant date.

STUDENT VISA (SUBCLASS 500)
Apply online at immi.homeaffairs.gov.au (ImmiAccount). Required documents: CoE, valid passport, OShC, Genuine Student (GS) Statement, financial evidence, academic transcripts, English test results. Visa fee: AUD 710 (non-refundable). Health examination at approved panel physician — schedule IMMEDIATELY after deciding to apply, takes 1–3 weeks and cannot be rushed. Biometrics required for some nationalities — check DHA website. Processing time: typically 4–8 weeks. Apply at least 8–12 weeks before course start. Financial evidence: proof you can cover full annual cost (tuition + AUD 21,041/year living costs + return airfare).

GENUINE STUDENT STATEMENT
A personal essay explaining why you want to study in Australia, career plans, and ties to home country. Critical for visa approval — weak GS Statement is a common rejection reason. Must demonstrate genuine intention to study and return home after graduation.

POST-STUDY WORK VISA (SUBCLASS 485)
2 years (Bachelor's degree graduates). 4+ years for regional study or specific STEM fields (verify current policy). Strong pathway to permanent residency. Engineering, IT, Healthcare on skilled migration occupation lists.

OFFICIAL SOURCES FOR VERIFICATION
immi.homeaffairs.gov.au — visa applications and GS Statement guidance
studyaustralia.gov.au — official Australian government study portal
ucat.ac.nz — UCAT ANZ for Medicine
cricos.teqsa.gov.au — verify CRICOS registration (mandatory check)
oshcworld.com.au — OShC provider comparison

Ireland.qualification_recognition:
QUALIFICATION RECOGNITION — IRELAND

Ireland is the only English-speaking country in the European Union — a unique combination giving international students an EU degree taught in English.

TWO APPLICATION ROUTES — NATIONALITY DETERMINES WHICH ONE:
This is the most confusing aspect of Ireland. Whether you use CAO or apply directly depends on nationality and fee status — NOT just where you are living.

ROUTE 1 — CAO (Central Applications Office):
Who uses this: EU/EEA citizens. UK nationals (special post-Brexit agreement). Students with qualifying EU/EEA fee status regardless of where they live.
Also: Non-EU students applying to Medicine (some medical schools require CAO number for HPAT registration).
Portal: cao.ie. Deadline: 1 February (main). Max choices: 10 Level 8 + 10 Level 7/6. Fee: €30 early / €45 normal / €65 late.
Admission based on CAO Points (grade conversion) + required tests. Ranked nationally.

ROUTE 2 — DIRECT TO UNIVERSITY:
Who uses this: Non-EU/non-EEA international students — India, Pakistan, UAE, Egypt, South Africa, Gulf countries.
How: Apply directly through each university's international office or online portal. No central system.
Deadline: January-May (varies per university — check each). Fee: ~€35-75 per application.
Admission: Each university assesses individually based on academic merit, English proficiency, personal statement, references, interviews where applicable.

FEE STATUS — CRITICAL CHECK FIRST:
Your fee status (EU or non-EU) determines which route you take AND how much tuition you pay. Based on nationality AND residency history — not just passport. An Indian national who has lived in an EU country for 3 of the past 5 years may qualify for EU fee status. Check with each university's admissions office before applying. Fee status is very rarely changed after enrolment.

WHICH QUALIFICATIONS ARE ACCEPTED:
IB DIPLOMA: Universally accepted at all Irish universities including TCD, UCD, UCC.
CAMBRIDGE A-LEVELS (2-3 subjects): Directly accepted. Most straightforward path for CAO points conversion.
INDIAN CBSE / ISC CLASS 12: Widely accepted by all Irish universities. Direct application route. Typically 65-80% minimum required — check per university.
PAKISTANI CAMBRIDGE A-LEVELS: Same as UK A-Levels. Strong grades required.
PAKISTANI FSc (HSSC): Generally accepted — check with each university. Some may request additional documentation.
SRI LANKA A/L: Generally accepted. BBC or better recommended.
UAE / GULF INTERNATIONAL SCHOOL (A-LEVELS OR IB): Fully accepted. Same standard as UK qualifications.
UAE TAWJIHI: Check per university — 90%+ typically required. Some universities prefer A-Levels/IB.
US HIGH SCHOOL DIPLOMA + SAT/ACT: Accepted at most — SAT 1200+ or ACT 27+ typically required.
FOUNDATION YEAR: Completing an Irish Foundation Year at a partner college guarantees or facilitates entry.
IGCSE ALONE: Not sufficient. IGCSE is Grade 10 equivalent. Must complete A-Levels or IB before applying.

SUBJECT REQUIREMENTS:
Ireland requires specific subjects — not just overall grades. Engineering requires Higher Level Mathematics. Science requires Chemistry or Biology. Missing a required subject can disqualify your application even with excellent overall grades. Always check specific subject requirements for each course before finalising A-Level or IB subject choices.

MEDICINE SPECIAL ROUTE:
HPAT (Health Professions Admission Test): Mandatory for EU/CAO route medicine applicants. 2.5-hour test. Register by 16 January (before CAO deadline). Take once per year in February. Results valid ONE YEAR only. Score combined with school leaving points for total entry score.
Non-EU international students applying directly to Irish medical schools follow a separate IUMC (Irish Universities Medical Consortium) international pathway — HPAT NOT required for direct route non-EU applicants. Check with each medical school.

ENGLISH LANGUAGE REQUIREMENTS:
IELTS Academic: 6.0-6.5 overall (no band below 5.5) standard. 6.5-7.0 for Medicine/Law.
TOEFL iBT: 80-90 standard. 90-100 for Medicine/Law.
PTE Academic: 55-65 standard. 65-73 for Medicine/Law.
Exemptions: Native English speaking country nationals. English-medium education entirely — confirm with each university. A-Level English grade C+ or IB English A grade 4+. India, Pakistan, UAE, Egypt: English proficiency test usually required even from English-medium international schools.

VISA AND FINANCIAL REQUIREMENTS:
Stamp 2 (Irish Student Permission): Required for non-EEA international students. Allows work up to 20 hours per week during term and 40 hours per week during holidays.
Proof of funds: €10,000 for living costs (6-month bank statement). Plus proof of tuition deposit payment (typically €2,000-5,000 paid before visa application).
Third Level Graduate Scheme (post-study work): Level 8 honours degree graduates receive 24 months to stay and work in Ireland. One of the most generous post-study schemes in Europe.
Ireland.admission_checklist:
APPLICATION ROUTES — TWO SYSTEMS
Non-EU international students (India, UAE, Pakistan, Gulf countries): Apply DIRECTLY to each university's international office — no central portal. Each university has its own online application system. Application fee: €35–75 per university. Deadline: January–May (varies per university — apply earlier for competitive programmes). Maximum applications: no limit.
EU/EEA students: Apply through CAO (cao.ie). Fee: €30 early bird (before 20 January) or €45 normal. Deadline: 1 February. Maximum 10 Level 8 + 10 Level 7/6 choices.

MEDICINE — SPECIAL RULES
HPAT: Required for Medicine in Ireland for EU/EEA pathway. Registration deadline: 16 January (must have CAO number first). Test dates: February. Register at hpat-ireland.acer.org. Non-EU international medical applicants: most universities use interview-based selection instead of HPAT — verify with each medical school. IUMC (iumc.ie): some non-EU students apply for Medicine through Irish Universities Medical Consortium rather than directly. RCSI has its own separate non-EU application process. Atlantic Bridge Programme: special application route specifically for North American (USA/Canada) students applying to Irish medical schools — allows single application to multiple Irish medical schools simultaneously.

ENGLISH LANGUAGE REQUIREMENTS
IELTS Academic: 6.0–6.5 standard; 7.0 for Medicine. TOEFL iBT: 80+ standard. Confirm exemption status if from English-medium international school — policies vary per university.

OFFER AND DEPOSIT
After receiving conditional offer, pay tuition deposit (typically €2,000–5,000) within 2–4 weeks. Deposit is mandatory for visa application — without payment receipt, Stamp 2 visa cannot be processed.

STAMP 2 VISA — IRISH STUDENT PERMISSION
Apply online at visas.inis.gov.ie. Required documents: offer letter, tuition deposit payment receipt, proof of €10,000 living funds (6-month bank statement), health insurance, valid passport. Processing time: apply 3 months before course start. After arrival: register at Garda National Immigration Bureau (GNIB) or local Garda station within 90 days and collect IRP (Irish Residence Permit) card — mandatory. Apply for PPS number (Personal Public Service Number) at local Intreo/social welfare office shortly after arrival — required for banking, working, tax, and healthcare.

FINANCIAL PROOF
€10,000 minimum for living expenses per year (shown via 6-month bank statement) PLUS tuition deposit receipt — both required for visa. Tuition fees: €10,000–30,000/year. Medicine: up to €60,000/year.

POST-STUDY WORK
Third Level Graduate Programme: 24 months stay-back permission after Level 8+ graduation. Strong pathway to Critical Skills Employment Permit after finding qualifying work.

OFFICIAL SOURCES FOR VERIFICATION
cao.ie — CAO application portal (EU/EEA)
inis.gov.ie — Irish immigration (Stamp 2 visa)
visas.inis.gov.ie — online visa application
hpat-ireland.acer.org — HPAT registration
iumc.ie — Irish Universities Medical Consortium
citizensinformation.ie — living and working in Ireland

Sweden.qualification_recognition:
QUALIFICATION RECOGNITION — SWEDEN

All applications to Swedish universities go through ONE portal: universityadmissions.se. This is simpler than Germany, France, UK, or USA — one platform, one fee, up to 8 bachelor's programmes.

CRITICAL DEADLINE: 15 January for autumn intake. Missing this deadline closes access to almost all English-taught programmes for that year. The second round is primarily for Swedish-taught programmes. No exceptions.
After submitting: approximately 2 weeks to upload supporting documents and pay application fee. Failure to do this means application may not be processed.

SELECTION GROUPS — HOW SWEDEN RANKS APPLICANTS:
Sweden does not use fixed cutoffs. When a programme has more applicants than seats, it ranks all qualified applicants by a MERIT SCORE calculated from school grades.
Group 1: Students with Swedish Gymnasieexamen. Compete against other Swedish students.
Group 2: Students with non-Swedish school leaving certificates (A-Levels, IB, CBSE, FSc etc.) — most international students. You compete against other international applicants — NOT against Swedish Group 1 students. A portion of seats is reserved for Group 2. This is a fairness mechanism giving international students a realistic chance.
Group 3: Work experience route — relevant mainly for vocational programmes.
Practical implication: Apply to a mix of competitive and less competitive programmes (up to 8 bachelor's) — you cannot predict your chances in advance.

WHICH QUALIFICATIONS ARE ACCEPTED:
IB DIPLOMA: Widely accepted. Minimum 24 points generally required. Competitive programmes require higher. IB Diploma covers both general and English requirements.
CAMBRIDGE A-LEVELS (2-3 subjects + GCSEs): Accepted at all Swedish universities. Programme-specific subject requirements still apply. English language requirement may be met through English A-Level.
INDIAN CBSE / ISC CLASS 12: Generally accepted — check per university at universityadmissions.se. Requires strong scores. Programme-specific requirements apply.
PAKISTANI CAMBRIDGE A-LEVELS: Widely accepted. Same treatment as Cambridge A-Levels from any country.
PAKISTANI FSc (HSSC): Check per university — may require Foundation Year for research university bachelor's.
SRI LANKA A/L: Generally accepted. Strong performance required. English A-Level or IELTS for English requirement.
UAE INTERNATIONAL SCHOOL (A-LEVELS OR IB): Fully accepted. Same treatment as standard qualifications.
UAE TAWJIHI: Check per university. May be accepted as basic school-leaving. Foundation Year may be recommended.
US HIGH SCHOOL DIPLOMA + SAT/AP: Generally accepted with SAT 1100+ or AP exams (3+ at grade 4-5).
IGCSE ALONE: Not sufficient. Must complete A-Levels or IB before applying to bachelor's programmes.

MATHEMATICS REQUIREMENT:
Students whose school grades were issued after 31 December 2009 must have completed mathematics equivalent to Swedish Mathematics 1a/1b/1c (basic university-level maths). Check on universityadmissions.se for your specific country.

TUITION AND FEES:
EU/EEA students: FREE — no tuition fees at Swedish public universities.
Non-EU/EEA students: SEK 80,000-200,000 per year (approximately €7,000-18,000). Fees introduced in 2011.
Application fee: SEK 900 (~€80) per semester application. Non-EU students must pay this — EU/EEA students may be exempt. Must be paid within ~2 weeks of application deadline. Failure to pay means application is not processed.
IMPORTANT: First tuition instalment must be paid BEFORE applying for residence permit.

ENGLISH LANGUAGE REQUIREMENTS:
All English-taught programmes require formal English proof — even native English speakers must provide documentation.
IELTS Academic: 6.5 overall (no section below 5.5) for bachelor's. 7.0 overall for master's/competitive.
TOEFL iBT: 90 overall for bachelor's. 100 for master's/competitive.
Cambridge C1 Advanced: C pass for bachelor's. B or above for competitive.
PTE Academic: 62 overall for bachelor's. 72 for competitive.
Exemptions: IB English A grade 4+ at SL or HL. A-Level English grade C or above.

RESIDENCE PERMIT AND VISA:
Non-EU students need a RESIDENCE PERMIT (not a traditional visa) to study in Sweden for more than 90 days. Apply through Migrationsverket (migrationsverket.se) ONLINE after paying first tuition instalment. Do not confuse with the admission process — they are separate steps.
Processing times have increased — apply as early as possible after paying tuition.
Sweden.admission_checklist:
APPLICATION PORTAL — SINGLE NATIONAL SYSTEM
Universityadmissions.se is the SINGLE portal for ALL Swedish university applications. One application, one fee, all universities. Apply to up to 8 bachelor's programmes or 4 master's programmes simultaneously. Register from mid-October. PRIMARY DEADLINE: 15 January (23:59 CET) — CRITICAL. Most English-taught programmes ONLY available in this first round. Missing this deadline means waiting a full year for most English-taught programmes. Spring intake (February start): very few English-taught programmes available — not recommended for most international students. Application fee: SEK 900 per semester. EU/EEA students may be exempt — verify status first. Pay within approximately 2 weeks of application deadline.

DOCUMENT SUBMISSION
Upload all supporting documents within approximately 2 weeks of application deadline. Required: school leaving certificate, transcripts, English language test results, certified translations if originals not in English or Swedish. Status will show 'Qualified' or 'Unqualified' — resolve any issues immediately.

SELECTION GROUPS
International students with foreign qualifications compete in Group 2 — against other international students, not against Swedish students who took specific Swedish tests. A percentage of seats is allocated to each group. Higher merit score = higher ranking = better chance of admission.

ENGLISH LANGUAGE REQUIREMENTS
IELTS Academic: 6.5 standard (English 6 equivalent); 7.0 for some master's (English 7 equivalent). TOEFL iBT: 90 standard. English A-Level grade C or above typically accepted in lieu of IELTS — confirm with specific programme.

TUITION FEES AND PAYMENT TIMING
EU/EEA students: FREE. Non-EU students: SEK 80,000–200,000/year (approximately €7,000–€18,000). CRITICAL: First tuition instalment must be paid BEFORE applying for residence permit — you cannot apply for the permit without payment confirmation. Pay immediately after accepting offer. Do not delay.

RESIDENCE PERMIT — NOT A TRADITIONAL VISA
Non-EU students require a residence permit (not a standard visa) for stays over 90 days. Apply online at migrationsverket.se AFTER paying first tuition instalment. Processing time: 1–4 months — apply immediately. Required documents: admission letter, proof of first tuition payment, proof of sufficient funds (SEK 8,515/month minimum), passport, passport photo. Application fee: SEK 1,000.

HOUSING — START SEARCHING EARLY
Student housing in Stockholm, Gothenburg, Lund/Malmö is VERY limited. University housing is oversubscribed. Private rental expensive (SEK 7,000–12,000/month for a room in Stockholm). Begin housing search immediately upon receiving admission — do not wait for permit. Apply for university housing the day you accept your offer.

FINANCIAL PROOF FOR RESIDENCE PERMIT
Minimum SEK 8,515 per month of study (approximately €760/month). Show for the full duration of your programme. SI (Swedish Institute) scholarship holders: scholarship letter serves as financial proof.

OFFICIAL SOURCES FOR VERIFICATION
universityadmissions.se — application portal
migrationsverket.se — residence permit applications
si.se — Swedish Institute scholarships
